name: White Beans, Greens & Sun-Dried Tomato Crosti
recipeIngredient: : Dried white beans, soaked overnight in refrigerator
Assorted greens (escarole, arugula, dandelion, or radicchio), well rinsed and coarsely chopped
Hot red pepper flakes, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the beans, rinse, and cover with 4 to 6 cups fresh water. Add the onion and all but 1 of the garlic cloves. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer for about 1 hour, until the beans are very soft.
3. Drain the beans, onions, and garlic. Reserve the remaining bean liquid.
4. Place the bean mixture in the container of a food processor and puree, adding enough cooking liquid to make a soft puree. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Chop the sun-dried tomatoes and soak them for 15 to 30 minutes in just enough bean cooking liquid to soften and release their flavor.
6. Fold the tomatoes into the beans. Fold in the yogurt.
7. In a large saute pan over high heat, wilt the greens in the vinegar. Drain well and season with salt, pepper, hot pepper flakes, and additional vinegar if desired.
8. To assemble the crostini, grill or toast the whole grain bread. Rub each slice with half of the remaining garlic clove. Spread the bread slices with white bean puree and top each with chopped greens.
Serving size: 1 slice
Calories: 152
Fat: 1.5 grams
Cholesterol: 1.1 milligrams
Sodium (without added salt): 247.4 milligrams
Source: Eat More, Weight Less
